dtcs.description: 31 and C1235/35 are for the front speed sensor RH. *

36 - Foreign Object is Attached on Tip of Front Speed Sensor LH The speed sensor detects wheel speed and sends the appropriate signals to the ECU. These signals are used to control the Multi-terrain ABS, EBD, BA, VSC, A-TRAC, CRAWL and hill-start assist control systems. This speed sensor contains a sensor IC, which consists of 2 MREs (Magnetic Resistance Element). The speed sensor rotor, which consists of 48 sets of N and S poles that are arranged in a circle, is integrated with the inner race of the hub bearing. To detect the rotation direction, the output waves are used to determine the relationship of the pulses that are generated by 2 MREs. Upon receiving this signal, the sensor IC outputs a forward or backward wave to the skid control ECU. HINT *

32 and C1236/36 are for the front speed sensor LH. NOTICE: When replacing the master cylinder solenoid, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (FR/FL SPEED OPEN) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and turn the Techstream on. (c) Enter the following menus: Chassis / ABS/VSC/TRAC / Data List. ABS/VSC/TRAC (d) Check for any momentary interruption in the wire harness and connector corresponding to the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Check for Intermittent Problems. OK: There are no momentary interruptions. HINT Perform the above inspection before removing the sensor and connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (FR/FL WHEEL SPEED, FR/FL WHEEL DIRECTION) (a) Check that there is no difference between the speed value output from the speed sensor displayed on the Techstream and the speed value displayed on the speedometer when driving the vehicle. ABS/VSC/TRAC OK: The speed value output from the speed sensor displayed on the Techstream is the same as the actual vehicle speed. (b) Check that the sensor signal conforms to the wheel direction. ABS/VSC/TRAC OK: The wheel direction is displayed correctly. NG --  INSPECT FRONT SPEED SENSOR INSTALLATION OK -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM SIGNAL CHECK (a) Check if test mode (signal check) DTCs are output. HINT Enter the following menus: Chassis / ABS/VSC/TRAC / Utility / Signal Check. Result B --  RECONFIRM DTC A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT FRONT SPEED SENSOR INSTALLATION (a) Check the speed sensor installation. OK: The installation bolt is tightened properly. There is no clearance between the sensor and front steering knuckle. Torque : 8.5 Nm (87 kgf-cm, 75 in-lbf) NOTICE: Check the speed sensor signal after replacement    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. NG --  INSTALL FRONT SPEED SENSOR CORRECTLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Wheel Speed Sensor/Service and Repair/Front Speed Sensor/Installation OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT SPEED SENSOR TIP (a) Remove the front speed sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Wheel Speed Sensor/Service and Repair/Front Speed Sensor/Removal. (b) Check the sensor tip. OK: No scratches or foreign matter on the sensor tip. NOTICE: Check the speed sensor signal after cleaning/replacement    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. NG --  CLEAN OR REPLACE FRONT SPEED SENSOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Wheel Speed Sensor/Service and Repair/Front Speed Sensor/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT FRONT SPEED SENSOR (a) Install the front speed sensor. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Disconnect the z17 and/or z18 front speed sensor connector. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: for RH for LH NG --  REPLACE FRONT SPEED SENSOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Wheel Speed Sensor/Service and Repair/Front Speed Sensor/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT SKID CONTROL SENSOR WIRE (a) Disconnect the z17 and Az2/z18 and Az1 skid control sensor wire connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: for RH for LH NOTICE: Check the speed sensor signal after replacement    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. dtcs.description: 33 and C1238/38 are for the rear speed sensor RH. *

39 - Foreign Object is Attached on Tip of Rear Speed Sensor LH The speed sensor detects wheel speed and sends the appropriate signals to the ECU. These signals are used to control the Multi-terrain ABS, EBD, BA, VSC, A-TRAC, CRAWL and hill-start assist control systems. This speed sensor contains a sensor IC, which consists of 2 MREs (Magnetic Resistance Element). The speed sensor rotor, which consists of 48 sets of N and S poles that are arranged in a circle, is integrated with the inner race of the hub bearing. To detect the rotation direction, the output waves are used to determine the relationship of the pulses that are generated by 2 MREs. Upon receiving this signal, the sensor IC outputs a forward or backward wave to the skid control ECU. HINT *

34 and C1239/39 are for the rear speed sensor LH. NOTICE: When replacing the master cylinder solenoid, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 66 - Steering Angle Sensor Zero Point Malfunction The skid control ECU acquires steering angle sensor zero point every time the engine switch is turned on (IG) and the vehicle is driven at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for approximately 5 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. If front wheel alignment or steering wheel position is adjusted without disconnecting the negative battery terminal, or if yaw rate and G sensor zero point is not acquired after the adjustments have been completed, the skid control ECU detects the difference between the previous zero point and newly acquired zero point and outputs this DTC to indicate a poor adjustment. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION OF YAW RATE AND G SENSOR (a) Perform zero point calibration of the yaw rate and G sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. HINT * When the stored zero point of the yaw rate and G sensor is cleared, steering angle sensor zero point will also be erased. * If the zero point and output value of the yaw rate and G sensor and the output value of the speed sensors are not normal, steering angle sensor zero point cannot be acquired normally even if the vehicle is driven straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK STEERING ANGLE SENSOR ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Drive the vehicle straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for at least 5 seconds. (b) Check that the centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set, while driving straight ahead. HINT If front wheel alignment and steering position are adjusted as a result of abnormal centered position of the steering wheel, acquire yaw rate and G sensor zero point again after the adjustments are completed. OK: The centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set. NG --  ADJUST FRONT WHEEL ALIGNMENT OR STEERING POSITION  See: Maintenance/Alignment/Service and Repair/Front Wheel Alignment OK --  END

dtcs.description: 11-C2115/15: Transmitter ID1 Operation Stop

15 - Transmitter ID5 Operation Stop The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the tire pressures of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured tire pressure values with the tire pressure threshold. When the measured tire pressure value is less than this threshold, the PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM FORCED TRANSMISSION OF TRANSMITTER ID OF ALL WHEELS (a) Set the tire pressures to the specified value(s)    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT * *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2125/25    See: C2121. * The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display tire pressure display screen. (f) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each tire at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (g) Check that each tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has changed. Result NOTICE: * It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. * When the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270° (including the spare tire). Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. (h) After confirming that all of the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has changed, set the tire pressure of all tires to the specified value(s). HINT If the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has not changed after rechecking, inspect the other problem (malfunction in transmission or reception function)    See: C2121. B --  END A --  GO TO TRANSMITTER AND RECEIVER INSPECTION PROCEDURE  See: C2121

(h) Check the Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT * *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2125/25    See: C2121. * The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display tire pressure display screen. Result NOTICE: * It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. * When "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then forcibly transmit the transmitter ID and recheck. * Record the transmitter ID and position of transmitters that are normal. (i) When an "ID Inflation Pressure" data value (one of ID1 to ID5) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. (j) When all of the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID5) has changed, identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter using the recorded ID numbers and output DTC. (k) Set the tire pressure of all tires to the specified value(s)    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 65-C2169/69: Abnormal Temperature Inside ID1 Tire

69 - Abnormal Temperature Inside ID5 Tire Each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter measures the internal temperature of its tire as well as tire pressure, and transmits the information to the tire pressure warning ECU along with the transmitter ID. If the measured temperature is out of the specified range, the tire pressure warning ECU recognizes it as a malfunction and stores DTCs, and the tire pressure warning light blinks for 1 minute and then remains on. HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 71 - Transmitter ID not Registered The IDs of each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter are registered to the tire pressure warning ECU. When the ECU detects that a transmitter ID code is not registered in the ECU, a DTC is stored. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CONFIRM REGISTRATION CONDITION (REGISTERED ID CODES) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. dtcs.description: 91 - Vehicle Speed Signal Error (Test Mode DTC) The tire pressure warning ECU receives speed signals from the combination meter via CAN communication. This DTC is stored upon entering test mode, and cleared when a vehicle speed signal of 20 km/h (12 mph) is output for 3 seconds or more. This DTC is stored only in test mode.
